revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Hero shrine]] * [[Martial temple]] and [[Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Gokoku shrines}}[[category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}}[[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Matsumae Gokoku Shrine-qq]] {{Gokoku shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Matsumae Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Matsumae Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Matsumae Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[ja:松前護國神]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}}[[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines?utm_source=chatgpt.com Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281?utm_source=chatgpt.com Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/?utm_source=chatgpt.com Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: completion categories]] {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: completion categories]] (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:11 It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also! == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion tier 2]] {{Gokoku shrines}} PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}}
