(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|{{PAGENAME}}|simple||ja|松前護國神社}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]]

It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]]

PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]]

Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:松前護國神社]]

PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple||ja|松前護國神社|12=draft}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松江護國神社]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]]

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple||ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松江護國神社]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松江護國神社]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松江護國神社]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:pages needing proper jawiki and history import]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} }} \ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|ja|松江護國神社|version=102010218|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} [[every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松江護國神社]] [[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:松前護國神]] [[sn:atsumae Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn: failed]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ko:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Pages with proper history]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation [[ja:%EB%A7%88%EC%93%B0%EC%97%90 %ED%98%B8%EA%B5%AD%EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}}
