revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q106852402]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|qid=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} (Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} revision.text:
[[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q106852402]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287}} (松前護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[:en:Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[:en:Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[:en:National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[:en:Eternal Spring Shrine|Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:The common end of myriad good deeds|The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References == {{reflist}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287}} (松前護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] [[ja:松前護國神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = | map_type = Japan | coordinates ={{Coord|41.441472222222224|N|140.11697222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | prefecture= {{ill|Matsumae|WD=Q1374364|1=Matsumae, Hokkaido}} }} {{ill|Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|松前護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Gokoku Shrine|qq=|WD=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} (松前護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is a Shinto shrine located in Matsumae, Hokkaido, Japan. Gokoku shrines, originally known as Shokonsha, were established during the Meiji Restoration to honor individuals who died in wars, serving as local branches of the central Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gokoku_Shrines Situated near Matsumae Castle, the northernmost castle in Japan, Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is part of a historic area that includes other notable sites such as Matsumae Shrine and the Buddhist temple Ryūun-in. https://mapcarta.com/W647365281 Visitors to Matsumae Gokoku Shrine can explore the rich history of the region, with the shrine serving as a place of remembrance for those who sacrificed their lives in service to the nation. The surrounding area offers a glimpse into Japan's feudal past, with Matsumae Castle and other cultural landmarks nearby. For those interested in visiting, the shrine is accessible by a short walk from the Matsuki bus stop. The address is 145 Matsushiro, Matsumae-chō, Matsumae-gun, Hokkaido. https://map.uu-hokkaido.jp/e/matsumae-shrine/ Please note that information about Matsumae Gokoku Shrine is limited, and visitors are encouraged to explore the site in person to fully appreciate its historical and cultural significance == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} [[ja:松前護國神社]] == References ==
